title: discopleura capillacea var. costata
intro: Discopleura capillacea var. costata, also known as the Brazilian ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Discopleura capillacea var. costata include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal infusions and salves.
